stem: A ball is dropped from an initial height of 30 feet and bounces off the ground repeatedly. The function h estimates that the maximum height reached after each bounce is 70% of the maximum height after the previous bounce. Which equation defines h, where h(n) is the estimated maximum height of the ball after it has hit the ground n times, and n is a whole number greater than 1 and less than 10?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The initial height is 30 feet, and after each bounce the maximum height is 70% (0.70 times) the previous maximum. After n bounces, the height is 30 multiplied by 0.70 n times, giving h(n) = 30(0.70)n.

Choices A and B use 0.30 (which would correspond to losing 70%, not retaining it). Choices B and C use the incorrect initial value 70 instead of 30.
